title: Islamic-Cultural Alignment KG
schema_name: Islamic-Cultural Mental-Health Alignment KG — Depth-First Tree Graph
fusion_method: deterministic Version 0.3 semantic-edge revision: All former IS_A relations were replaced by explicit, domain-meaningful KG predicates. Depth is represented through compositional and operational relations (for example HAS_COMPONENT, USES_TECHNIQUE, MEASURES, TARGETS, MAINTAINS, MODIFIES, and REQUIRES_MONITORING), while cross-branch relations encode clinically relevant dependencies, treatment logic, contextual constraints, and cultural-spiritual adaptations. Predicate-vocabulary compaction: repeated branch-specific predicates were consolidated into reusable semantic relations such as HAS_COMPONENT, HAS_MANIFESTATION, HAS_MODALITY, HAS_THERAPY_APPROACH, USES_TECHNIQUE, MODIFIES, TARGETS, MAINTAINS, INFORMS, and REQUIRES_MONITORING. This preserves graph meaning while reducing predicate redundancy. Predicate-vocabulary compaction: repeated branch-specific predicates were consolidated into reusable semantic relations such as HAS_COMPONENT, HAS_MANIFESTATION, HAS_MODALITY, HAS_THERAPY_APPROACH, USES_TECHNIQUE, MODIFIES, TARGETS, MAINTAINS, INFORMS, and REQUIRES_MONITORING. This preserves graph meaning while reducing predicate redundancy.

node_types.description: Person-specific cultural, language, family, faith-salience, and care-preference context. It is descriptive, voluntary, and never inferred solely from demographic proxies. Extraction guidance: Extract only explicit preferences, narratives, practices, values, and contextual facts. Do not infer religiosity or culture from origin, name, language, or diagnosis.

Non-coercive adjustment to communication, engagement, care delivery, or support that improves acceptability without replacing indicated clinical care. Extraction guidance: Extract an adaptation only when explicitly recommended, requested, or agreed. Link its clinical action in the clinical KG and maintain its cultural/faith details here. Constraints: An adaptation never independently establishes diagnosis or medication choice. Activation requires patient relevance, consent, clinical review, and evidence or qualified-expert provenance. Adaptation must not override urgent safety escalation or replace indicated clinical care.
